title: Docs Workflow Gap Followups #713-#718
goal: Implement documentation follow-ups #713-#718 from the workflow documentation review: align startup guidance, expose planning lifecycle commands, fix the stale routing reference, document sequential lane execution, document post-new-plan tightening, and clarify implement --changed projection inspection.
non_goals: Do not redesign the planning model beyond the documented gaps.
Do not add a workflow-log contract; the maintainer marked that as a one-off request.
Do not introduce multi-lane umbrella execplans; preserve lane boundaries.

intent.outcome: Close #713-#718 with bounded documentation and guidance updates.
intent.constraints: Keep scope bounded to docs and command guidance for the reviewed gaps; preserve sequential lane execution as the intended model.
intent.latitude: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.proof: Focused workspace CLI tests, lint, planning doctor, maintainer surfaces, root CLI authority check, and make check passed.

invariants: One roadmap lane is the broader planning owner; one or more execplans may implement slices under that lane.
Do not document or emit guidance that encourages unrelated lanes to share one umbrella execplan.
The compact CLI route remains primary for ordinary startup.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make the docs and CLI guidance steer agents into the intended planning workflow without broad rereading or multi-lane batching.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Create issues for the adjusted documentation gaps, then ingest and implement them.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Turn the reviewed documentation gaps into tracked issues and land a bounded documentation correction pass.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Update startup, command-map, candidate-lane, execplan, and implementer-context guidance, plus any matching summary/planning CLI text.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.required validation commands: rg checks for stale references and multi-lane umbrella wording; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; make check if code or generated guidance changes.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not add workflow-log docs; do not endorse multi-lane umbrella execplans; keep changes documentation/guidance scoped.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

invariants: Sequential roadmap lane execution is the default.
Execplans implement bounded slices under one lane; they do not aggregate unrelated lanes.
Compact CLI output remains the first-choice orientation surface.
validation_commands: rg -n "planning-routing-contract|umbrella execplan|combined.*lane|all planned lanes" .agentic-workspace docs src tests
completion_criteria: Issues #713-#718 are addressed in docs and matching CLI guidance.
Stale planning-routing reference is removed or replaced.
Startup guidance has one dominant compact CLI-first path.
Planning lifecycle commands are discoverable from the command map.
Sequential lane execution is documented; multi-lane umbrella execplans are discouraged.
